title: SignalAtom
description: The structured research object produced by signal_atoms.py. The model fills the reading and consequence fields; the ethics fields (allowed_use, risk_level, ethical_use_status, citation_required) are set from the source registry, never by the model. An atom stores original analysis and a citation, never the full text of a source. No em dashes in any value.

title.description: The signal's title or headline, as published.

source.description: The outlet or source name, matching a registry record.

url.description: The public link to the original.

medium.description: Art form or subject, from taxonomy.json.

speaker_type.description: Who is speaking in the signal.

artist_voice_score.description: How directly this is an artist's own voice rather than a reporter's.

psychological_need.description: The need underneath, from the psychological lens in taxonomy.json.

social_tension.description: The social or institutional tension at play.

now_signal.description: What this says about the present.

studio_consequence.description: What a working artist should understand or do.

money_consequence.description: Who profits, who pays, who is exposed.

human_contact_consequence.description: What it means for connection or collaboration.

ethical_use_status.description: Set from the registry. Forbidden atoms are never published.

allowed_use.description: Set from the registry. How this source may be used.

risk_level.description: Set from the registry. Red is never ingested.

citation_required.description: Always true. Every atom carries its source forward.
